architecture.summary: This is a comprehensive survey of Large Language Model agents that combines LLM reasoning capabilities with environment perception, decision-making, and autonomous action. The survey organizes the field into four categories: reasoning-enhanced agents (chain-of-thought, tree-structured deliberation), tool-augmented agents (external APIs, knowledge bases), multi-agent systems (inter-agent communication), and memory-augmented agents (persistent context). Information flows from LLM reasoning through various augmentation mechanisms (tool calls, memory retrieval, inter-agent messages) to support autonomous task execution across software engineering, scientific research, embodied robotics, and web automation domains.

biblio.title: Large Language Model Agents: A Comprehensive Survey on Architectures, Capabilities, and Applications

claims.statement: LLM agents represent a paradigm shift in artificial intelligence, combining the remarkable reasoning capabilities of foundation models with the ability to perceive environments, make decisions, and take actions autonomously
Current agents achieve impressive performance on structured tasks
Significant challenges remain in areas such as long-horizon planning, hallucination mitigation, and safe deployment
The survey evaluates agents on established benchmarks including SWE-bench, WebArena, and AgentBench
Reasoning-enhanced agents leverage chain-of-thought and tree-structured deliberation
Tool-augmented agents extend LLM capabilities through external APIs and knowledge bases
Multi-agent systems enable collaborative problem-solving through inter-agent communication
Memory-augmented agents maintain persistent context across interactions
